overview.short: Valara (Malayalam: വാളറ) is located in between Neriamangalam on the eastern border of the Ernakulam District and Adimali of Kochi - Madurai National Highway (NH-49).
overview.description: Valara (Malayalam: വാളറ) is located in between Neriamangalam on the eastern border of the Ernakulam District and Adimali of Kochi - Madurai National Highway (NH-49). It is in Idukki district, Kerala, at a distance of 14 km from Adimali. The main attraction of this place is Valara waterfalls. Which is in the Deviyar river, a small river originating from western ghats, passing through Adimali Grama Panchayath.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Athirappilly
topPlaces.description: Athirappilly is a Grama Panchayat with 489.00 km2 area in Chalakudy Taluk, Thrissur district in Kerala, India. It is located 52 km from Thrissur city, 60 km northeast of Kochi city, 32 km northeast of Cochin International Airport, and 24 km from Chalakudy town.

topPlaces.name: Ayyampuzha
topPlaces.description: Ayyampuzha is a village and panchayat in Angamaly, Ernakulam district, in the Indian state of Kerala. Kalady plantations are a part of the panchaayat.

topPlaces.name: Athirappilly Falls
topPlaces.description: Athirappilly Falls (Malayalam: [ɐd̪iɾɐpːiɭːi]) is situated in Athirappilly Panchayat in Chalakudy Taluk of Thrissur District in Kerala, India on the Chalakudy River, which originates from the upper reaches of the Western Ghats at the entrance to the Sholayar ranges. It is the largest waterfall in Kerala, which stands tall at 81.5 feet.

topPlaces.name: Charpa Falls
topPlaces.description: Charpa Falls is a waterfall located in Athirappilly panchayath in Thrissur district of Kerala. Located on the west-flowing Chalakudy River, this waterfall lies in between the more famous Athirappilly Falls and Vazhachal Falls.

items.note: Athirappilly is a Grama Panchayat with 489.00 km2 area in Chalakudy Taluk, Thrissur district in Kerala, India. It is located 52 km from Thrissur city, 60 km northeast of Kochi city, 32 km northeast of Cochin International Airport, and 24 km from Chalakudy town.
Ayyampuzha is a village and panchayat in Angamaly, Ernakulam district, in the Indian state of Kerala. Kalady plantations are a part of the panchaayat.

Athirappilly Falls (Malayalam: [ɐd̪iɾɐpːiɭːi]) is situated in Athirappilly Panchayat in Chalakudy Taluk of Thrissur District in Kerala, India on the Chalakudy River, which originates from the upper reaches of the Western Ghats at the entrance to the Sholayar ranges. It is the largest waterfall in Kerala, which stands tall at 81.5 feet.

Charpa Falls is a waterfall located in Athirappilly panchayath in Thrissur district of Kerala. Located on the west-flowing Chalakudy River, this waterfall lies in between the more famous Athirappilly Falls and Vazhachal Falls.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Valara?
faq.a: The best time to visit Valara is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (20 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (44 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Valara?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Valara at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Valara?
faq.a: Stays in Valara start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Valara?
faq.a: The top attractions are Athirappilly, Ayyampuzha, Athirappilly Falls, Charpa Falls.
